---
title: "LRPDID11: Waitlist-crossover arm-by-wave sensitivity for phonetic spelling (SPPHON) (P), off-floor prevalence"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by an LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).

Substantially revised, including correction of the floor-estimand provenance, by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPDID11 jointly models whether each child is off the phonetic-spelling floor at t1, t2 and t3, with a separate immediate-minus-waitlist gap at each wave. It is a longitudinal sensitivity analysis alongside the randomised floor-rule ITT (LRPITT09), not an independent replication.

::: {.callout-important title="The estimand is off-floor prevalence, not floor exit"}

Phonetic spelling is heavily floored (a large share of period post-scores are
zero). On the graded count scale a handful of dispersed tail values, not the arm
contrast, would drive the fit, so the observation is a **Bernoulli on the binary
"off-floor" indicator** (period post > 0), with no dispersion parameter.

The quantity is **off-floor prevalence**, `Pr(score > 0)` at each wave over every observed child — **not** the floor-exit transition `Pr(t2 > 0 | t1 = 0)` that #119 defines as the ITT floor-rule's **post-hoc exploratory headline**. The two are materially different: a child already off floor who stays off counts identically to one who newly moves off, so prevalence blends “already off and stayed” with “came off”. LRPITT09 carries the exploratory transition analysis on the single randomised t1→t2 phase. Read these arm-by-wave contrasts as differences in *being* off floor, never as “coming off the floor” or as a graded gain.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="What this estimand is, and is not"}

`arm_gap_t1` checks pre-treatment prevalence balance. `tau_t2` is the randomised immediate-minus-waitlist t2 log-odds contrast. `arm_gap_t3` and `delta_crossover = tau_t2 - arm_gap_t3` are post-crossover associations because both arms are treated by t3 with different histories.

**Why prevalence and not floor exit.** Restricting every wave to children floored at its start would make the immediate arm's P2 risk set depend on its own P1 treatment: children already moved off floor would be removed. Modelling prevalence at all three waves avoids that post-treatment risk-set selection but answers a different question from the ITT's post-hoc exploratory floor-exit headline.

**No period-start outcome.** A `gamma_own` term would condition P2 on t2, which is treatment-affected for the immediate arm. Instead, t1 is the first response wave. The child random intercept partially pools stable differences; it does not exactly difference them away.

::: {.callout-warning title="Log-odds and risk-difference scales differ"}
The coefficients are additive on conditional log-odds. Equal log-odds gaps generally imply different probability gaps at different baseline risks, so each risk-difference translation is standardized at its own wave and covariate distribution. `delta_crossover` on the logit scale is not a probability-scale cross-difference (Puhani 2012, [doi:10.1016/j.econlet.2011.11.025](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.econlet.2011.11.025)).
:::

Read `tau_t2` alongside LRPITT09, remembering that it concerns prevalence rather than the ITT's baseline-floored floor-exit subgroup. The remaining contrasts are post-crossover descriptions.

:::

## Model

With $\eta_{i,t} = \alpha + \beta_{\text{period},t} + \text{arm\_gap}_{t}G_i + \gamma_A A_{t1,\text{std},i} + u_{\text{child},i}$:

$$
[\,P_{i,t} > 0\,] \sim \text{Bernoulli}\big(\sigma(\eta_{i,t})\big).
$$

Here $G_i=1$ for the immediate arm, $\beta_{\text{period},t1}=0$, and $\text{arm\_gap}_{t}=(\text{arm\_gap}_{t1},\tau_{t2},\text{arm\_gap}_{t3})$. The intercept is a learned offset around the pooled t1 prevalence logit. There is no dispersion $\kappa$ under the Bernoulli. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_did.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
